label: Operational Intelligence for AlloyDB by Google Cloud

branding.tagline: Monitor database health, identify query bottlenecks, and track historical performance trends directly from Looker in minutes.

CONNECTION_NAME.label: Looker Connection Name
CONNECTION_NAME.description: The name of the Looker connection configured to access the AlloyDB 'postgres' administrative database.

DATABASE_NAME.label: Target Application Database
DATABASE_NAME.description: The exact name of the primary application database you want to monitor (e.g., 'global_gadgets_demo'). The dashboards will filter to this database by default.

STATEMENTS_SCHEMA.label: pg_stat_statements Schema
STATEMENTS_SCHEMA.description: The schema where the pg_stat_statements extension was created inside the postgres database. (Default: 'public')

SCRATCH_SCHEMA.label: Time-Series Scratch Schema
SCRATCH_SCHEMA.description: The schema Looker uses to build the permanent history tables for Time-Series trends. The connection user must have write access to this schema. (Default: 'temp_looker_schema')

PII_QUERY_TEXT.label: Mask Sensitive SQL Queries?
PII_QUERY_TEXT.description: Security toggle. Type 'SHOW' to view full raw SQL queries. Type 'HIDE' to completely mask all SQL query text from Looker users.

MAX_CONNECTIONS.label: AlloyDB Max Connections Limit
MAX_CONNECTIONS.description: The maximum number of connections allowed by your AlloyDB instance. Used to calculate Connection Saturation KPIs. (Default: '1000')
